exam.name: NCLEX-RN
exam.category_description: Master the clinical judgment and safety-first nursing knowledge needed to pass licensure exams and practice confidently from day one.
exam.hero_headline: Pass NCLEX-RN with clinical judgment that sticks.
exam.hero_subheadline: Exam-aligned nursing study paths built from the official NCSBN test plan and designed for long-term retention.
exam.companion_problem: Most prep material is fragmented, too broad, or disconnected from the exact NCLEX-RN blueprint.
exam.companion_solution: Only Ever maps the official test plan into structured 15-minute topics and retention-first notes so you learn exactly what the exam expects.

sections.title: Management of Care
sections.description: Providing and directing nursing care that enhances the care delivery setting to protect the client and health care personnel.

topics.title: Advance Directives, Self-Determination, and Life Planning
topics.description: Incorporate advance directives, self-care determination, and life planning into client care.
learning_objectives: Assess client and staff member knowledge of advance directives.
Integrate advance directives into the client plan of care.
Provide client with information about advance directives, self-care determination, and life planning.

topics.title: Advocacy
topics.description: Act in the role of client advocate and support client decisions.
learning_objectives: Discuss treatment options respecting client decisions.
Provide advocacy information to staff and utilize resources appropriately.
Support unbiased treatment and equal access to care regardless of culture, sexual orientation, or gender identity.

topics.title: Assignment, Delegation and Supervision
topics.description: Delegate tasks safely and supervise care provided by others.
learning_objectives: Identify tasks for assignment based on client needs.
Delegate appropriately utilizing the five rights of delegation.
Supervise care provided by LPN/VNs and assistive personnel, evaluating correct completion.

topics.title: Case Management
topics.description: Coordinate care to help clients achieve and maintain independence.
learning_objectives: Explore resources to assist clients in achieving independence.
Assess the need for materials, equipment, and cost-effective care.
Initiate, evaluate, and update individualized care plans.

topics.title: Client Rights
topics.description: Ensure client rights are respected, including the right to refuse treatment.
learning_objectives: Recognize the client's right to refuse treatment and procedures.
Discuss treatment options and decisions with the client.
Provide education and evaluate understanding about client rights and responsibilities.

topics.title: Collaboration with Multidisciplinary Team
topics.description: Work effectively with health care team members to provide comprehensive care.
learning_objectives: Identify the need for interdisciplinary conferences.
Report significant information to other disciplines to ensure continuity of care.
Collaborate with multidisciplinary team members and serve as a resource person.

topics.title: Concepts of Management
topics.description: Apply management concepts to address client problems and manage staff.
learning_objectives: Identify roles and responsibilities of health care team members.
Act as a liaison between the client and others to coordinate care.
Manage conflict among clients and staff and evaluate management outcomes.

topics.title: Confidentiality and Information Security
topics.description: Maintain client confidentiality and privacy in all forms of communication.
learning_objectives: Assess staff and client understanding of confidentiality requirements.
Maintain privacy in all contexts, including social media and disclosure of information.
Intervene appropriately when staff members have breached confidentiality.

topics.title: Continuity of Care
topics.description: Ensure seamless transitions in care through effective communication and procedures.
learning_objectives: Provide and receive handoff of care (report) on assigned clients.
Perform procedures necessary to safely admit, transfer, and discharge a client.
Follow up on unresolved issues regarding client care.

topics.title: Establishing Priorities
topics.description: Apply pathophysiology to establish priorities for multiple clients.
learning_objectives: Apply knowledge of pathophysiology when establishing priorities for interventions.
Prioritize the delivery of client care based on acuity.
Evaluate and revise the plan of care for multiple clients as needed.

topics.title: Ethical Practice
topics.description: Recognize ethical dilemmas and practice consistently with the nurses' code of ethics.
learning_objectives: Recognize and report ethical dilemmas affecting client care.
Practice in a manner consistent with the nurses' code of ethics.
Evaluate outcomes of interventions aimed at promoting ethical practice.

topics.title: Informed Consent
topics.description: Understand and participate in the informed consent process.
learning_objectives: Identify the appropriate person to provide informed consent for the client.
Describe the components of informed consent and provide written materials.
Verify the client receives education and consents for care and procedures.

topics.title: Information Technology
topics.description: Utilize information technology and facility regulations to manage client care securely.
learning_objectives: Receive, verify, and implement health care provider orders.
Apply knowledge of facility regulations when accessing client records and databases.
Enter computer documentation accurately, completely, and in a timely manner.

topics.title: Legal Rights and Responsibilities
topics.description: Provide care within the legal scope of practice and understand legal issues.
learning_objectives: Identify legal issues affecting the client, including limitations of self and others.
Report client conditions as required by law (e.g., abuse, neglect, communicable diseases).
Provide care strictly within the legal scope of practice.

topics.title: Performance Improvement and Quality Improvement
topics.description: Participate in performance improvement projects and quality assurance activities.
learning_objectives: Define performance improvement and quality assurance activities.
Participate in performance improvement projects and utilize evidence-based references.
Evaluate the impact of performance improvement measures on client care.

topics.title: Referrals
topics.description: Assess the need for referrals and connect clients with community resources.
learning_objectives: Assess the need to refer clients for assistance and obtain necessary orders.
Identify community resources appropriate for the client.
Identify which documents to include when referring a client.

sections.title: Safety and Infection Prevention and Control
sections.description: Protecting clients and health care personnel from health and environmental hazards.

topics.title: Accident, Error, and Injury Prevention
topics.description: Prevent accidents and injuries by assessing risks and implementing safety measures.
learning_objectives: Assess client for allergies and intervene as needed.
Identify factors that influence accident and injury prevention such as age or mental status.
Protect client from injury and promote staff and workplace safety.

topics.title: Emergency Response Plan
topics.description: Participate in disaster planning and emergency response activities.
learning_objectives: Determine which clients to recommend for discharge in a disaster situation.
Identify nursing roles and use clinical decision-making during emergencies.
Participate in emergency planning, response, and disaster drills.

topics.title: Ergonomic Principles
topics.description: Apply ergonomic principles to prevent repetitive stress injuries.
learning_objectives: Assess client ability to balance, transfer, and use assistive devices prior to planning care.
Provide instruction about body positions that eliminate repetitive stress.
Use ergonomic principles safely when providing care.

topics.title: Handling Hazardous and Infectious Materials
topics.description: Identify and safely handle biohazardous, flammable, and infectious materials.
learning_objectives: Identify biohazardous, flammable, and infectious materials in the environment.
Follow procedures and demonstrate safe handling techniques for hazardous materials.
Ensure safe implementation of internal radiation therapy.

topics.title: Home Safety
topics.description: Assess and educate clients on home safety and environmental hazards.
learning_objectives: Assess the need for client home modifications for safety.
Apply knowledge of client pathophysiology to home safety interventions.
Educate clients on safety concerns and protective equipment.

topics.title: Reporting of Incident, Event, Irregular Occurrence, or Variance
topics.description: Acknowledge, document, and report practice errors and incidents.
learning_objectives: Identify situations where reporting of incidents or variances is appropriate.
Acknowledge and document practice errors and near misses.
Report, intervene, and escalate unsafe practice of staff as necessary.

topics.title: Safe Use of Equipment
topics.description: Inspect, maintain, and educate on the safe use of health care equipment.
learning_objectives: Inspect equipment for safety hazards before and during use.
Facilitate appropriate and safe use of equipment, and teach clients about safe usage.
Remove malfunctioning equipment from care areas and report issues.

topics.title: Security Plan
topics.description: Use critical thinking in situations related to security planning and protocols.
learning_objectives: Use clinical decision-making in situations related to security planning.
Apply principles of triage and evacuation procedures.
Follow security plans and procedures such as newborn nursery security or controlled access.

topics.title: Standard Precautions, Transmission-Based Precautions, and Surgical Asepsis
topics.description: Apply infection prevention principles and evaluate infection control precautions.
learning_objectives: Apply principles of infection prevention including hand hygiene, isolation, and sterile technique.
Understand communicable diseases and the modes of organism transmission.
Educate clients and staff regarding infection prevention measures.

topics.title: Use of Restraints and Safety Devices
topics.description: Assess appropriateness and monitor the use of restraints and safety devices.
learning_objectives: Assess appropriateness of the type of restraint or safety device used.
Follow all legal and facility requirements when using restraints.
Monitor and evaluate client response to restraints.

sections.title: Health Promotion and Maintenance
sections.description: Providing and directing nursing care that incorporates the knowledge of expected growth and development principles, prevention, and strategies to achieve optimal health.

topics.title: Aging Process
topics.description: Provide care and education recognizing expected age-related changes across the lifespan.
learning_objectives: Assess client's reactions to expected age-related changes.
Provide age-appropriate care and education for clients from birth through adulthood.
Provide specialized care and education for adult clients ages 65 years and over.

topics.title: Ante-, Intra-, Postpartum and Newborn Care
topics.description: Assess and provide comprehensive care to clients during pregnancy, labor, and postpartum.
learning_objectives: Assess psychosocial response to pregnancy and symptoms of postpartum complications.
Calculate expected delivery date and check fetal heart rate.
Provide prenatal, intrapartum, postpartum care, and assist with newborn care.

topics.title: Developmental Stages and Transitions
topics.description: Identify expected physical, cognitive, and psychosocial stages of development.
learning_objectives: Identify expected physical, cognitive, and psychosocial stages of development.
Compare client development to expected milestones and report deviations.
Assist clients to cope with life transitions such as parenting, puberty, or retirement.

topics.title: Health Promotion and Disease Prevention
topics.description: Educate clients on actions to promote health and prevent disease.
learning_objectives: Assess and educate clients about health risks based on family, population, and community.
Educate clients on actions to promote and maintain health and prevent disease.
Assist clients in maintaining an optimal level of health and evaluate understanding.

topics.title: Health Screening
topics.description: Apply pathophysiology and perform comprehensive health and risk assessments.
learning_objectives: Apply knowledge of pathophysiology to health screening.
Perform targeted screening assessments (e.g., vision, nutrition, depression).
Use appropriate procedures and interviewing techniques when obtaining a health history.

topics.title: High-Risk Behaviors
topics.description: Assess lifestyle risks and educate on the prevention and treatment of high-risk behaviors.
learning_objectives: Assess client lifestyle practice risks that may impact health.
Assist clients to identify behaviors and risks that impact well-being.
Educate clients about prevention and treatment of high-risk health behaviors.

topics.title: Lifestyle Choices
topics.description: Assess lifestyle choices, sexuality, and contraception needs.
learning_objectives: Assess client's lifestyle choices, attitudes on sexuality, and desire for contraception.
Identify contraindications and expected outcomes for family planning methods.
Educate clients on sexuality issues and evaluate alternative health care practices.

topics.title: Self-Care
topics.description: Assess client ability to manage care at home and assist with self-care goals.
learning_objectives: Assess client ability to manage care in the home environment.
Consider client self-care needs before developing or revising care plans.
Assist primary caregivers working with the client to meet self-care goals.

topics.title: Techniques of Physical Assessment
topics.description: Apply nursing procedures and psychomotor skills to perform comprehensive health assessments.
learning_objectives: Apply knowledge of nursing procedures to techniques of physical assessment.
Choose physical assessment equipment appropriate for the client.
Perform comprehensive health assessments.

sections.title: Psychosocial Integrity
sections.description: Providing and directing nursing care that promotes the emotional, mental, and social well-being of the client experiencing stressful events or mental illness.

topics.title: Abuse or Neglect
topics.description: Assess, report, and provide interventions for abused or neglected clients.
learning_objectives: Assess clients for abuse or neglect and report or escalate as necessary.
Identify risk factors for domestic, child, and elder abuse or neglect.
Provide a safe environment and counsel recipients of abuse on coping strategies.

topics.title: Behavioral Interventions
topics.description: Assess behavior and assist clients in achieving and maintaining self-control.
learning_objectives: Assess client appearance, mood, and psychomotor behavior for inappropriate responses.
Assist clients to develop and use strategies to decrease anxiety.
Incorporate behavioral management techniques and orient the client to reality.

topics.title: Chemical and Other Dependencies and Substance Use Disorder
topics.description: Assess and plan care for clients experiencing substance use disorder or withdrawal.
learning_objectives: Assess clients for substance misuse, toxicities, and reactions to substance-related diagnoses.
Plan and provide care for clients experiencing withdrawal, toxicity, or non-substance dependencies.
Educate clients on substance use diagnoses and encourage participation in support groups.

topics.title: Coping Mechanisms
topics.description: Assess support systems and the client's ability to adapt to role and life changes.
learning_objectives: Assess client's support systems, available resources, and ability to cope with life changes.
Provide support to clients with an unexpected altered body image.
Evaluate client's constructive use of defense mechanisms and adaptation to role changes.

topics.title: Crisis Intervention
topics.description: Identify clients in crisis and use crisis intervention techniques to assist in coping.
learning_objectives: Identify the client in crisis and assess the potential for violence.
Apply knowledge of client psychopathology to crisis intervention techniques.
Guide the client to resources for recovery from crisis situations.

topics.title: Cultural Awareness and Cultural Influences on Health
topics.description: Incorporate client cultural practices and beliefs into the plan of care.
learning_objectives: Assess the importance of client self-reported culture/ethnicity when planning care.
Respect and incorporate client cultural practices and beliefs into the provision of care.
Evaluate and document how client language needs were met.

topics.title: End-of-Life Care
topics.description: Assist clients and families in coping with end-of-life issues and interventions.
learning_objectives: Assess client's ability to cope with end-of-life interventions and identify end-of-life needs.
Recognize the need for and provide psychosocial support to the family/caregiver.
Provide end-of-life care and education to clients.

topics.title: Family Dynamics
topics.description: Assess family functioning, parental techniques, and integrate new members.
learning_objectives: Assess barriers and stressors that impact family functioning.
Assess client support systems and parental discipline techniques.
Assist client to integrate new members into family structures.

topics.title: Grief and Loss
topics.description: Provide care, support, and education to clients experiencing anticipatory grieving and loss.
learning_objectives: Provide care for a client experiencing grief or loss.
Support the client in anticipatory grieving and inform them of expected reactions.
Provide resources to adjust to loss and evaluate the client's coping.

topics.title: Mental Health Concepts
topics.description: Identify symptoms of acute and chronic mental illness and apply psychopathology concepts.
learning_objectives: Identify signs of impaired cognition and recognize acute and chronic mental illness symptoms.
Assess client for alterations in mood, judgment, cognition, and reasoning.
Apply knowledge of psychopathology to mental health concepts in therapy.

topics.title: Religious and Spiritual Influences on Health
topics.description: Assess and meet client emotional and self-reported spiritual needs.
learning_objectives: Identify emotional problems related to self-reported religious/spiritual beliefs.
Assess and plan interventions that meet client emotional and spiritual needs.
Evaluate whether client's self-reported religious/spiritual needs are met.

topics.title: Sensory and Perceptual Alterations
topics.description: Assist clients experiencing visual, auditory, or cognitive sensory alterations.
learning_objectives: Identify time, place, and stimuli surrounding the appearance of sensory symptoms.
Provide appropriate, nonthreatening, and nonjudgmental care for sensory alterations.
Assist clients to develop strategies for dealing with sensory and thought disturbances.

topics.title: Stress Management
topics.description: Assess stressors and implement measures to reduce environmental stressors.
learning_objectives: Recognize nonverbal cues to physical and psychological stressors.
Implement measures to reduce environmental stressors.
Provide information to clients on stress management techniques and evaluate their use.

topics.title: Support Systems
topics.description: Assist families to plan care and encourage involvement in healthcare decisions.
learning_objectives: Assist families to plan care for clients with impaired cognition.
Encourage client involvement in the health care decision-making process.
Evaluate client feelings about the diagnosis and treatment plan.

topics.title: Therapeutic Communication
topics.description: Assess communication needs and use therapeutic communication techniques effectively.
learning_objectives: Assess verbal and nonverbal client communication needs.
Use therapeutic communication techniques, allowing time to communicate.
Encourage clients to verbalize feelings and respect personal values.

topics.title: Therapeutic Environment
topics.description: Promote a therapeutic environment and make appropriate room assignments.
learning_objectives: Identify external factors that may interfere with client recovery.
Make client room assignments that support the therapeutic milieu.
Promote a consistently therapeutic environment.

sections.title: Basic Care and Comfort
sections.description: Providing comfort and assistance in the performance of activities of daily living.

topics.title: Assistive Devices
topics.description: Assess the need for and assist clients in the correct use of assistive devices.
learning_objectives: Assess client difficulty with communication and sensory functions to determine the need for assistive devices.
Assist clients to compensate for physical or sensory impairments using devices.
Manage clients using prostheses and evaluate the correct use of assistive devices.

topics.title: Elimination
topics.description: Assess and manage clients with alterations in bowel and bladder elimination.
learning_objectives: Assess and manage clients with an alteration in bowel and bladder elimination.
Perform irrigations and provide skin care to incontinent clients.
Use alternative methods to promote voiding and evaluate restoration of ability to eliminate.

topics.title: Mobility and Immobility
topics.description: Identify complications of immobility and implement measures to promote circulation.
learning_objectives: Identify complications of immobility and perform skin assessments.
Apply, maintain, or remove orthopedic devices and maintain correct body alignment.
Implement measures to promote circulation and evaluate responses to interventions.

topics.title: Nonpharmacological Comfort Interventions
topics.description: Assess need for alternative therapies and provide nonpharmacological comfort measures.
learning_objectives: Assess client needs for alternative therapies and palliative care.
Assess client pain and incorporate alternative therapies to provide nonpharmacological comfort.
Evaluate client responses to nonpharmacological and palliative interventions.

topics.title: Nutrition and Oral Hydration
topics.description: Evaluate nutritional status, maintain special diets, and provide tube feedings.
learning_objectives: Assess client ability to eat, hydration status, and nutritional status.
Provide and maintain special diets based on diagnoses and cultural considerations.
Provide client nutrition through tube feedings and evaluate intake and output.

topics.title: Personal Hygiene
topics.description: Assess personal hygiene routines and provide necessary adaptations for daily living.
learning_objectives: Assess client personal hygiene habits and performance of activities of daily living.
Provide adaptations for activities of daily living to promote independence and dignity.
Perform postmortem care.

topics.title: Rest and Sleep
topics.description: Assess sleep patterns and apply pathophysiology to rest and sleep interventions.
learning_objectives: Assess client's sleep and rest patterns and intervene as needed.
Apply knowledge of pathophysiology to execute rest and sleep interventions.
Schedule client care activities to promote adequate rest.

sections.title: Pharmacological and Parenteral Therapies
sections.description: Providing care related to the administration of medications and parenteral therapies.

topics.title: Adverse Effects, Contraindications, Side Effects, and Interactions
topics.description: Identify contraindications, side effects, and incompatibilities of prescribed medications.
learning_objectives: Identify contraindications, incompatibilities, and allergic reactions to medications.
Assess and provide information on side effects and interactions.
Evaluate and document client responses taken to counteract adverse medication effects.

topics.title: Blood and Blood Products
topics.description: Check venous access, document necessary information, and administer blood products safely.
learning_objectives: Identify client according to facility policy prior to blood product administration.
Check appropriate venous access and administer blood products.
Evaluate client response to blood products and document necessary information.

topics.title: Central Venous Access Devices
topics.description: Access, maintain, and educate clients on the care of central venous access devices.
learning_objectives: Educate clients on the reason for and care of venous access devices.
Access and maintain central venous access devices safely.
Provide continuous care for a client with a central venous access device.

topics.title: Dosage Calculations
topics.description: Perform necessary calculations for medication administration using critical thinking.
learning_objectives: Perform accurate mathematical calculations needed for medication administration.
Use clinical decision-making when calculating drug dosages to ensure client safety.

topics.title: Expected Actions and Outcomes
topics.description: Obtain information on prescribed medications and evaluate client use and response over time.
learning_objectives: Use clinical decision-making to address expected outcomes of medications across various routes.
Obtain and review formulary information regarding prescribed medications.
Evaluate client's adherence and response to medication over time.

topics.title: Medication Administration
topics.description: Prepare, administer, and document medications safely using the rights of administration.
learning_objectives: Educate clients on medications and self-administration procedures.
Prepare and safely administer medications by common and parenteral routes using the rights of medication administration.
Titrate dosages, participate in medication reconciliation, and dispose of medications safely.

topics.title: Parenteral and Intravenous Therapies
topics.description: Prepare, monitor, and evaluate intravenous therapies and infusion pumps.
learning_objectives: Identify appropriate veins and prepare clients for peripheral venous access device insertion.
Monitor the use of infusion pumps and maintain intravenous infusion sites.
Evaluate the client's response to intermittent parenteral fluid therapy.

topics.title: Pharmacological Pain Management
topics.description: Assess need for and administer pharmacological pain management safely.
learning_objectives: Assess client needs for PRN pain medications across varying routes.
Administer pharmacologic pain management appropriate for client age and diagnosis.
Handle controlled substances within regulatory guidelines and evaluate pain medication response.

topics.title: Total Parenteral Nutrition
topics.description: Educate, administer, and evaluate client response to Total Parenteral Nutrition (TPN).
learning_objectives: Educate clients on the need for and use of TPN.
Identify side effects related to TPN and intervene appropriately.
Administer TPN using appropriate psychomotor skills and evaluate client response.

sections.title: Reduction of Risk Potential
sections.description: Reducing the likelihood that clients will develop complications or health problems related to existing conditions, treatments or procedures.

topics.title: Changes and Abnormalities in Vital Signs
topics.description: Assess, respond to, and apply pathophysiology when measuring changes in vital signs.
learning_objectives: Assess and respond to changes and trends in client vital signs.
Apply necessary psychomotor skills when assessing vital signs.
Apply pathophysiology knowledge to the interpretation of vital signs.

topics.title: Diagnostic Tests
topics.description: Apply nursing procedures, compare findings, and perform diagnostic testing.
learning_objectives: Perform testing within the scope of practice, such as ECGs or fetal monitoring.
Compare diagnostic findings with pretest results.
Monitor results of diagnostic tests and maternal/fetal diagnostics, intervening as needed.

topics.title: Laboratory Values
topics.description: Obtain specimens, monitor laboratory values, and compare findings to normal values.
learning_objectives: Obtain blood and non-blood specimens safely.
Compare client laboratory values to normal parameters.
Educate clients about lab tests and notify primary health care providers of critical results.

topics.title: Potential for Alterations in Body Systems
topics.description: Identify client potential for complications such as aspiration, skin breakdown, and poor perfusion.
learning_objectives: Identify potential for client aspiration, skin breakdown, and insufficient vascular perfusion.
Educate clients on methods to prevent complications associated with diagnoses.
Compare current data to baseline and monitor outputs for significant changes.

topics.title: Potential for Complications of Diagnostic Tests, Treatments, and Procedures
topics.description: Assess and monitor clients for abnormal responses and complications following procedures.
learning_objectives: Assess for abnormal responses following diagnostic tests or procedures.
Position clients properly and use precautions to prevent procedure-related complications.
Insert, maintain, or remove tubes and catheters safely to prevent complications.

topics.title: Potential for Complications from Surgical Procedures and Health Alterations
topics.description: Apply pathophysiology to monitor for and prevent surgical complications.
learning_objectives: Apply knowledge of pathophysiology when monitoring for postoperative complications.
Evaluate client responses to interventions aimed at preventing aspiration and promoting venous return.

topics.title: System-Specific Assessments
topics.description: Perform risk and focused assessments for specific body systems and health alterations.
learning_objectives: Perform focused assessments on specific systems, such as evaluating abnormal peripheral pulses or neurologic status.
Perform risk assessments for factors like sensory impairment, fall risk, and delayed wound healing.
Recognize changes in client conditions and intervene effectively.

topics.title: Therapeutic Procedures
topics.description: Assess response to anesthesia, educate on home management, and monitor therapeutic devices.
learning_objectives: Assess client response to local, regional, or general anesthesia.
Monitor effective functioning of therapeutic devices such as chest tubes or continuous bladder irrigation.
Manage clients during and following a procedure with moderate sedation.

sections.title: Physiological Adaptation
sections.description: Managing and providing care for clients with acute, chronic or life-threatening physical health conditions.

topics.title: Alterations in Body Systems
topics.description: Assess adaptation to illness, monitor tube drainage, and identify infectious diseases.
learning_objectives: Assess client adaptation to health alterations, illnesses, and diseases.
Provide care for clients experiencing complications of pregnancy, labor, or delivery.
Monitor and maintain internal monitoring devices, wounds, and drainage equipment.

topics.title: Fluid and Electrolyte Imbalances
topics.description: Identify signs, apply pathophysiology, and manage fluid and electrolyte imbalances.
learning_objectives: Identify signs and symptoms of fluid and electrolyte imbalances.
Manage the care of clients with fluid and electrolyte imbalances utilizing pathophysiological principles.
Evaluate the client's response to interventions aimed at correcting imbalances.

topics.title: Hemodynamics
topics.description: Assess for decreased cardiac output, identify arrhythmias, and manage hemodynamic status.
learning_objectives: Identify cardiac rhythm strip abnormalities and manage clients on telemetry.
Intervene to improve cardiovascular status based on pathophysiology of hemodynamics.
Manage clients receiving hemodialysis, continuous renal replacement, and pacing devices.

topics.title: Illness Management
topics.description: Educate clients, implement interventions, and manage recovery from acute or chronic illness.
learning_objectives: Educate clients regarding acute or chronic conditions and illness management.
Promote continuity of care and evaluate the effectiveness of treatment plans.
Manage the care of a client with impaired ventilation and oxygenation.

topics.title: Medical Emergencies
topics.description: Apply knowledge of pathophysiology and procedures to manage and document medical emergencies.
learning_objectives: Perform emergency care procedures applying relevant nursing skills and psychomotor skills.
Notify primary health care providers about unexpected client responses in emergency situations.
Evaluate and document client responses to emergency interventions such as restoration of breathing or pulse.

topics.title: Pathophysiology
topics.description: Understand general principles and identify pathophysiology related to conditions.
learning_objectives: Identify pathophysiology related to acute or chronic conditions.
Understand general principles of pathophysiology, including injury, repair, immunity, and cellular structure.

topics.title: Unexpected Response to Therapies
topics.description: Assess and intervene for unexpected adverse responses to client therapies.
learning_objectives: Assess client for unexpected adverse responses to therapy such as increased intracranial pressure or hemorrhage.
Recognize signs and symptoms of client complications and intervene efficiently.
Promote recovery of a client from unexpected responses to therapy.
